findings.message: `assert s is s` is a tautology — Python's identity operator always holds for the same variable binding. This test can never fail regardless of any code change to Settings.
findings.suggestion: Replace with a meaningful assertion, e.g. `assert isinstance(s, Settings)` to at least verify construction succeeds, or delete the test entirely since `test_defaults` already exercises the constructor.

findings.message: The test patches the exact property under test (`resolved_log_level`) with a fixed value (`'MOCKED'`), then asserts that the mock returns `'MOCKED'`. It exercises only `unittest.mock`, never the real implementation. It will pass even if `resolved_log_level` is deleted.
findings.suggestion: Remove the mock entirely and assert real behaviour, e.g. `assert Settings(environment='prod').resolved_log_level == 'INFO'`. The existing `test_resolved_log_level_is_debug_in_dev` and `test_resolved_log_level_is_info_outside_dev` already cover this — the new test is redundant even if fixed.
